waves.title: Freeze the authored-wave architecture baseline
Bootstrap the Rust workspace and operator command surface
Implement typed config, authored-wave parsing, and dark-factory lint
Build planning status and control-plane bootstrap
Implement the Codex-backed launcher and agent lifecycle manager
Build the right-side operator panel in the TUI
Make dark-factory an enforced execution profile
Implement autonomous next-wave scheduling and dependency gating
Capture traces and validate replay semantics
Dogfood the Rust system on this repository
Land the Wave 0.2 authority core
Land the reducer-backed planning and projection spine
Land result envelopes and proof lifecycle
Land scheduler authority and the wave worktree contract
Land true parallel-wave execution and merge discipline
Land runtime policy and multi-runtime adapters

authority.projection_source: planning status, queue/control JSON, and operator-facing status surfaces are reducer-backed projections over canonical scheduler authority plus compatibility run records; proof and closure surfaces are envelope-first, and replay remains compatibility-backed

runtime_boundary.executor_boundary: runtime-neutral launch spec plus adapter registry in wave-runtime
runtime_boundary.selection_policy: explicit executor runtime selection with default codex and authored fallback order
runtime_boundary.fallback_policy: fallback only when the selected runtime is unavailable before meaningful work starts

closure_overrides.reason: promotion conflict accepted after Wave 15 runtime-policy work was reconciled in the root workspace
closure_overrides.detail: The live rerun proved the runtime boundary locally, then failed only at promotion because the same Wave 15 files were already edited in the root workspace. This override waives the failed promotion without rewriting the failed run record so dependent waves can proceed.
